text:
Hao Wu
Hao Wu is a Lecturer in the English for Academic Purposes program at NYU Shanghai. Prior to joining NYU Shanghai, Hao taught English for academic purposes, English as a foreign language, and second language acquisition to both undergraduates and postgraduates in China and the United Kingdom for five years.
Select Publications
Wu, H. (2025). English for academic purposes: Learn, apply, succeed. [Open-access Textbook]. Pioneer Publications. https://doi.org/10.54475/eap1
Wu, H. (2019). A corpus-based analysis of TESOL EFL students’ use of logical connectors in spoken English. Theory and Practice in Language Studies, 9(6), 625-636. https://doi.org/10.17507/tpls.0906.04
Education
MSc, Applied Linguistics for Language Teaching (Distinction)University of Oxford
MA, Teaching English to Speakers of Other LanguagesUniversity of Hong Kong
BA, EnglishUniversity of Science and Technology Liaoning
Research Interests
Public Speaking/Oral Presentation in L2
Language Learner Psychology
English Medium Instruction
Educational Technology
EAP 100/101: Exploring Medical Knowledge
EAP 101: The Dawn After Doomsday
